subject: Public Health
title: The Global Tobacco Epidemic
description: One-minute skill: The Global Tobacco Epidemic.

hook.text: 1.3 billion people use tobacco worldwide—and it will kill half of them unless they quit.

buildup.text: Tobacco kills 8 million people per year—through cancer, heart disease, stroke, and chronic lung disease.

discovery.text: Secondhand smoke kills 1.3 million non-smokers annually—including children exposed at home.

twist.text: The tobacco industry knew smoking caused cancer by the 1950s but suppressed the evidence for decades.

climax.text: The WHO Framework Convention on Tobacco Control—the first global health treaty—commits 182 countries to action.

punchline.text: The only industry that kills its best customers—and recruits new ones.

quiz.question: How many people does secondhand smoke kill annually?
